Name: [Full Name]
Title: [Job Title/Position]
Bio: [Brief bio description - one sentence]
Image: [Profile image URL or path]
Location: [City, State/Country]
Expertise: [Comma-separated list of expertise areas]
Email: [email@domain.com]
Twitter: [@handle]
LinkedIn: [https://linkedin.com/in/profile]

---

[Extended biography content goes here. This section can contain multiple paragraphs describing the author's background, experience, achievements, and expertise.

You can include:
- Career highlights
- Educational background
- Notable work or publications
- Personal interests
- Professional philosophy

This content will be displayed in the "About" section of the author's profile page.]

EXAMPLE:
================

Name: Sarah Johnson
Title: Senior Business Reporter
Bio: Former TechCrunch senior writer specializing in creator economy trends and digital business models
Image: https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1494790108755-2616c395d75b?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&crop=face
Location: San Francisco, CA
Expertise: Creator Economy, Business Analysis, Startup Coverage, Digital Marketing
Email: sarah.johnson@influencernews.com
Twitter: @sarahjtech
LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/in/sarahjohnson

---

Sarah Johnson brings over 8 years of technology journalism experience to Influencer News, where she covers the intersection of creativity and commerce in the digital age. Before joining our team, she was a senior writer at TechCrunch, where she specialized in covering emerging trends in the creator economy and digital business models.

Her work has been featured in major publications including Wired, Fast Company, and The Information. Sarah has a particular expertise in analyzing platform monetization strategies and their impact on content creators. She has interviewed hundreds of successful creators, from micro-influencers to major YouTube stars, giving her unique insights into the challenges and opportunities facing digital entrepreneurs.

Sarah holds a Master's degree in Digital Media from Stanford University and a Bachelor's in Journalism from UC Berkeley. When she's not writing about the latest creator economy developments, she enjoys photography and hosts a weekly podcast about sustainable business practices in the digital space.

Her reporting has helped break several major stories about platform policy changes and their effects on creator communities, making her one of the most trusted voices in creator economy journalism.
